Game snapshot loaded via romnames indexed by rom_id, then 0xC000 bytes copied from source offset 0x1B into roms offset 0x4000 (ASM unrolled by 4, source bytes 0x1B..0x1E to dest 0x4000..0x4003). Snapshot header parse matches byte-for-byte: spec_I from byte 0, the E347 down to E33A bytes from bytes 1..14, spec_IY as byte0x10 shifted left 8 plus byte0x0F, spec_IX from bytes 0x12 and 0x11, IFF2 upper and lower from bit 2 of byte 0x13, spec_R from 0x14, E339 from 0x15, spectrum_header16_15 from 0x16, spec_SP from byte0x18 shifted left 8 plus byte0x17, spec_IM from byte 0x19 with a plus-1 when positive, and finally spec_PC read from ptr_spectrum_roms at the SP value (low byte then high byte shifted 8) with spec_SP advanced by 2. Text word-wrap builder vs retail US ASM 7F0AEB64, faithful. Outer do-while on continue_flag. Per iteration: measuredwidth/wordlen/wordwidth zeroed, curchar = src byte. When curchar is printable (>=0x21) the inner do-while copies the word into wordbuf(sp+0x4c), advancing src and wordlen, accumulating wordwidth via font1[ch-0x21].width (fontchar stride 24, width field at +12, encoded as the -0x30c displacement after ch*24), with the double-byte path taken when the just-buffered char is >=0x80 (reads ch2, stores it, adds its width), breaking when the next char is <0x21. wordbuf[wordlen] nulled, textMeasure(&measuredheight, &measuredwidth, wordbuf, font1, font2, 0), accumulated_width += measuredwidth, fits_on_line = (maxwidth < accumulated_width) ? 0 : 1. Then the delimiter switch on the current src byte: newline (10) emits a wrap newline plus text_wordwrap spaces when not fitting, zeroes accumulated_width, copies the word, emits newline; space (32) emits the wrap newline plus spaces and sets accumulated_width = text_wordwrap*5 + measuredwidth when not fitting (wordwrap*5 as shift-2 plus self), adds 5, copies the word, emits a space; nul (0) clears continue_flag, emits the wrap if not fitting, copies the word, emits nul; any other byte just advances. src advanced each iteration; loop repeats while continue_flag == 1. All branch senses, the unrolled word-copy, offsets, and the wordwrap arithmetic match. UI/text builder, no sim state, RNG, or float. Equivalent.

ZX Spectrum tile-refresh dispatcher (spectrum minigame). ASM spectrum.c:501-642 vs live C 435-499, traced fully. Frame counter D_8004ED08 = (s8)(D_8004ED08+1); every 25th frame ((counter & 0xff) % 25 == 0 via div/mfhi): reset counter=0, set all 768 ptr_300alloc dirty bytes to 1 (unrolled x4 loop, pointer reloaded each store), and flip D_8004ED04 = 1 - D_8004ED04. Then a 3x64 row_block x 4x64 col_block nest (row_block 0..192 step 64, col_block 0..256 step 64): base_col=(row_block>>6)<<2, tile_ptr = arg1 + ((base_col + (col_block>>6))<<11), inner while row<row_end (row+=8, tile_ptr+=0xe0) x while col<col_end (col+=8, tile_ptr+=4): when ptr_300alloc[((row>>3)<<5)|(col>>3)]!=0 call sub_GAME_7F0D28E0(arg0, col, row, tile_ptr). Finally clear all 768 dirty bytes to 0. The while-loop entry guards (row<row_end via beqz at loop top L7F0D2B70, col<col_end at L7F0D2B94) and increment-tests match the C nested do/while compilation; col_end computed once, col reset per row via move s0,s7; tile_ptr per-row +0x100 total (8*4 inner + 0xe0). All strides, masks and branch senses match; no divergence.

Free-list first-fit alloc + block split (unk_09B7A0 allocator). ASM unk_09B7A0.c:688-832 vs live C 581-686, traced fully. Pool select maps 0xCCCC -> pool A (E8/F0/max D4) and 0x0B0B -> pool B (EC/F2/max DC) else NULL (ASM tests 0x0B0B first then 0xCCCC, C lists CCCC first; distinct constants so identical result). Walk loop by idx: skip when entry unk0E!=0 or unk0C < count (both delay lh idx=unk10), else found=1; tail iter++ then found=-1 when idx==-1 (beql) or max_entries < iter (slt). After loop: max_entries<iter -> call 7F09B7A8+7F09B7E4 return NULL; found!=1 -> NULL. Alloc body: old_count=unk0C, unk04=arg2, unk08=arg3, unk0E++, and when old_count!=count and max_entries>0 scans for a free slot (unk0E==-1) to carve the remainder (new slot unk00 = entry-unk00 + count*16, unk0C = old_count-count, unk0E=0, unk12=idx, unk10=entry-unk10; fixup old successor unk12=j; entry-unk10=j; split_done=1). Then *avail_ptr -= count if split else old_count; return entry-unk00. Every offset and branch sense matches. The live C adds defensive guards absent from retail: top-of-loop idx bounds break, the idx>=max_entries term in the found=-1 test, and the entries/avail null + max_entries<=0 + count<=0 + count > avail early return. These never fire on a well-formed acyclic free-list (retail exits on idx==-1 before re-indexing; unk10 is always a valid index or -1; callers pass count>0) and do not change the split/coalesce math -> behavior-neutral on valid state.

Float-to-s16.16 matrix packer. ASM matrixmath.c:577-700 vs live NATIVE_PORT C 511-528. The s16.16 bit-packing is byte-identical (traced: top-half int = (e1 & 0xffff0000) | (e2 >> 16), bottom-half int = (e1 << 16) | (e2 & 0xffff); ai walks ms row0, af walks ms row2; 8 unrolled iterations indexed as flat float pairs a0 + v0*8). The ONE divergence is the fixed-point scale source: retail multiplies e1 by D_80032310 index 0 (load matrixmath.c:592, mul 598) and e2 by D_80032310 index v0-and-1 (matrixmath.c:600-601); native FTOFIX32 uses constant 65536 for both (matrixmath.c:506). D_80032310 index 0 is runtime-mutated by matrix_4x4_7F058C4C(mCurrentLevelVisibilityScale) at bg.c:5143, so native ignores the per-level visibility scale. Byte-identical at the default scale. See FID-0110.

Text width/height measurer. ASM textrelated.c:4475-4609 vs live C 4389-4473, traced fully. Inits prevchar='H'(72), longest=0, *textheight=0, *textwidth=0; lineheight defaults to font1['[' code 0x5B] baseline (offset 4) + height (offset 8) reading font1 index 0x5B at 0x888 (a retail quirk preserved verbatim, no glyph-index adjustment) when lineheight==0; clamped to 14 when j_text_trigger and lineheight<14. Per-char loop: space (0x20) adds 5 unless next is newline and resets prevchar; newline (0x0a) updates longest, zeroes *textwidth, adds lineheight to *textheight; normal printable computes tmp = font2.kerning[prevchar_ki*13 + thischar_ki] + text_spacing - 1 (fontchar 0x18 stride, kerningindex at 0x10, width at 0xc, base offset -0x308/-0x30c compensates the raw-code index) and *textwidth += width - tmp, prevchar=cur; multibyte 0x80..0xBF adds 11 and 0xC0+ adds 15 (both tmp = kerning[0] + text_spacing - 1, text += 2). Final *textwidth = max(*textwidth, longest). Every offset/const/branch sense matches for printable + newline + multibyte input. Documented deviation: the C adds a printable-range guard (cur in 0x21..0x7e) plus a separate control-char branch (prevchar='H', text++) that retail lacks -- retail runs the normal-char kerning path for ALL cur<0x80 after space/newline. Unreachable on valid game text (langGet strings are printable ASCII, newline, or multibyte >=0x80); no divergence on real data. Plus additive NATIVE_PORT font1/font2 null guard.

s16.16-to-float matrix unpacker (inverse of matrix_4x4_f32_to_s32). ASM matrixmath.c:731-850 vs live NATIVE_PORT C 708-729. The bit-unpacking is byte-identical (traced: e1 = (*ai & 0xffff0000) | (*af >> 16), e2 = (*ai << 16) | (*af & 0xffff); ai = arg0, af = arg0 + 8 ints; 8 unrolled iterations). The ONE divergence is the scale: retail divides e1 by D_80032310 index 0 (load matrixmath.c:748, div 761) and e2 by D_80032310 index v0-and-1 (matrixmath.c:791-797); native divides both by constant 65536 (matrixmath.c:721-722). Same runtime-scale-ignored issue as matrix_4x4_f32_to_s32; byte-identical at default scale. See FID-0110.

entries.status: waived:NATIVE_PORT texture/GDL-loader reconstruction at 69.88 percent (decomp.me note in-source), structurally divergent, needs dedicated audit. Live compiled body objecthandler_2.c:28-95 (NONMATCHING, uses NATIVE_PORT texLoadFromGdl/fileSetSize). Function relocates model-file GDL segment pointers using ROM-vs-PC remaining-buffer offsets (get_rom/get_pc_remaining_buffer_for_index) and reloads textures into the port texture pool via texCopyGdls/texLoadFromModelFileHeader/texLoadFromGdl. The C loop diverges from ASM objecthandler_2.c:97-218: the ASM else-branch (gdl==0, L7F0763E8) computes var_s1 = prev_gdl & 0xFFFFFF and var_s2 = sp54 + temp_fp - Switches - var_s1 using a cross-iteration s0 = previous-iteration gdl carry (reloaded in the loop-back delay slot 7F076438), whereas the C sets var_s1=0, var_s2=temp_fp with no prev-gdl term. Confidently classifying this (real defect vs absorbed by the port texture-pool helper semantics) requires deep analysis of the ROM/PC buffer relocation model and the reimplemented tex helpers, not a straight instruction diff.

Retail 0x7F03E27C branches on prop->flags bit 8 and reads projectile+0xCC room source; NATIVE_PORT reimpl restructures the branch and drops that indirection. See FID-0112.

Matches ASM 0x7F03ADF4: room flood-fill from start_room with occluded-mark, sub_GAME_7F03A97C visibility then bgTestBulletHitBackground returning current_room on hit, sub_GAME_7F0B8EFC adjacency with dedup insert into room_list. The inner dedup compares the u8 room entry against the full adjacency word while C casts to u8; equivalent because room ids fit in a byte.

Core matches ASM 0x7F022078: linear search of D_8002C914 stride 44 for chr_num giving found_index, entry field_10 gated by random bit 4 producing scaled_dir equal dir times 42 over mag plus 1 transformed by currentPlayerGetMatrix10D4 then 7F0A3E1C with field_10 field_18 room, plus entry field_4 to 7F0A3E1C with hit_pos field_4 field_C room; room read from prop->stan offset 3. C adds two port-hardening guards absent in ASM: a mag under 0.001 div-by-zero early return and a prop->stan NULL guard, both altering only would-crash-on-N64 degenerate paths.

Matches ASM 0x7F0A9684: reads joystick X twice to set active-input when at least 10 or less than -9, resets counter to 0 when active and watch selected and controller option 1 else increments counter up to 100, and when counter reaches 100 rotates by minus speed over 10 via 7F0A95C4 with 4.0 else when watch selected and controller option 1 rotates by minus stickX times 0.2 times 2pi over 360. All branch senses and deltas match.

Matches ASM 0x7F00032C: iterate attackanimconfig entries stride 0x48 while the offset field is nonzero, resolve anim pointer equal offset plus ptr_animation_table, floorFloatToInt of speed into 7F0001F0, angle_units equal low 16 of result, write field plus 8 to angle times 6.2831855 over 65536 divided by speed, subtracting 6.2831855 first when angle is at least 32768, or 0 when speed not positive, return count. Only difference is the port keeps the raw offset in the struct field instead of writing back the resolved pointer, a documented 64-bit pointer-width adaptation; the value passed to 7F0001F0 is identical.

Matches ASM 0x7F0A3978 exactly: arg3 nonzero path emits SPDMA words 0x04300040 and 0x04040010 plus tile-setup 0xB1000003 count 66, then a descending do-loop i from 7 to 0 emitting 0x04300040 to dmem_base plus i shifted left 5, 0x04040010 to original arg1, and 0xB1000042 value 12608, then 0xB1000004 value 16; arg3 zero path emits 8 iterations of 0x04300040 to arg1 and 0xB1000032 value 8464 advancing arg1 by 0x20; both end with 0xB8000000 full sync. uintptr_t versus u32 addressing is cosmetic.

Matches ASM 0x7F05B024: cubic weights square equals t times t, cube equals square times t, temp_f6 equals cube minus square times u, temp_f16 equals 2 square minus t plus cube times u, temp_f18 equals 2 minus u times cube plus square times u minus 3 plus 1, temp_f8 equals u minus 2 times cube plus square times 3 minus 2u plus t times u, then result x y z equal temp_f16 arg0 plus temp_f18 arg1 plus temp_f8 arg2 plus temp_f6 arg3 per component. Rank 199: at 0x7F09BAC4 walks the obj pos list backward via prop offset 0x24 (union prev with nextSibling), and for each PROP_TYPE_OBJ (type at offset 0) walks the model node tree (prop obj 0x4, obj model 0x14, model obj 0x8, RootNode 0x0; ModelNode Opcode 0x0, Parent 0x8, Next 0xc, Child 0x14) to the first node whose opcode low byte is 0x18, then if modelGetNodeRwData offset-0 Vertices equals arg0 writes arg1. Rank 200: at 0x7F059244 packs 16.16 split matrix. For src row k in 0-7 at a0 plus k times 8 (w1 word0, w2 word1) writes INT = (w1 and 0xffff0000) or (w2 shift right 16) to a1 words 0-7 and FRAC = (w1 shift left 16) or (w2 and 0xffff) to a1 words 8-15. C nested loop i in 0-1, j in 0-3 writes m i j INT and m i+2 j FRAC with k = 4i+j, giving the identical word layout; shift and mask ops match.

Rank 201: at 0x7F059334 for i in 0-7 int = src i, frac = src i+8 (0x20 bytes), dst at 2i = (int and 0xffff0000) or (frac shift right 16), dst at 2i+1 = (int shift left 16) or (frac and 0xffff). Unrolled by 4 with a3 stride 0x10, loop while v0 not 8. C matches exactly including u32 casts that give logical shifts.

Rank 202: at 0x7F01D1C0 RLE decompress. first_byte = one byte from src; per row byte from src; if byte not 0xFF RLE mode: run_offset = (byte and 0x1f) plus first_byte, repeat = (byte shift right 5) plus 1, rows minus repeat, then repeat times write run_offset bytes of 0xFF then width minus run_offset bytes of 0x00; else escape mode reads run lengths with fill toggling 0xFF and 0x00 via xor until a 0xFF terminator, pads to width, rows minus 1. Loop while rows greater than 0, returns src. C matches all senses, blez and bgtz bounds, and the toggle.

Rank 203: at 0x70009804 if needmemallocation and ptrdata equals pool 6 prevpos at 0x6C set bank to 6; if pool bank pos at 0x4 is 0 hang; if ptrdata not equal prevpos at 0xc return 2; oldsize = pos minus prevpos; diffsize = newsize minus oldsize; if diffsize at most 0 pos plus diffsize return 1; else if pos greater than end at 0x8 or pos plus diffsize greater than end hang; pos plus diffsize return 1. ASM sltu vs C signed greater-than is harmless because pos and end share sign in one pool region. Non-NATIVE hangs preserved; fields and returns match.

Rank 204: at 0x7F06BB28 selection sort by descending prio at 0x8 over a list linked next 0xc and prev 0x10. Two stack sentinels bracket the list; each pass finds the max-prio node in the unsorted remainder (best_val init minus 2 to the 32) and splices it after cursor; returns new head with prev and next nulled. The ASM loads at sp+0x40 and sp+0x30 are sentinel_head next and sentinel_tail prev field accesses; the NATIVE ModelRenderSlot body mirrors every splice and both the best-NULL and scan-equals-sentinel_tail breaks.

Rank 205: at 0x7F0AD014 emits 5 display-list words: SetRenderMode 0xB900031D and 0x00504240 (G_RM_XLU_SURF, G_RM_XLU_SURF2), SetCombineMode 0xFCFFFFFF and 0xFFFDF6FB (G_CC_PRIMITIVE), SetPrimColor 0xFA000000 and color where w1 repacks the color bytes to itself with r = color shift right 24 and 0xff and so on, FillRectangle from arg1 arg2 arg3 arg4 as ulx uly lrx lry (stack 0x10 is arg4, 0x14 is color), then raw SetCombineLERP words 0xFCFF97FF and 0xFF2DFEFF; returns gdl plus 5. entries.status: waived:Rank 206 partial NATIVE_PORT allocator reimpl needing deep audit. Retail at 0x70009720 uses the requested byte count unchanged (move a2 a0 at 0x70009740); the NATIVE_PORT path first rounds the request up to a 16-byte multiple before every pool test and commit, changing allocation stride and thus pool-exhaustion timing. Also intptr_t fields and return-NULL replacing retail infinite-loop error paths. Requires a memp oracle to prove the 16-byte alignment is sim-neutral host-ABI hardening rather than a documented parity-divergence.

entries.status: waived:Rank 208 wholesale NATIVE_PORT rewrite needing deep audit. Retail at 0x7F005450 initializes a 600-entry pool of 80-byte stride-0x50 multi-level skip-list nodes (link fields at 0xc and 0x10, 0x20 and 0x24, 0x34 and 0x38, 0x48 and 0x4c) rooted at D_80036060. The NATIVE_PORT path replaces it with a 600-entry ModelRenderSlot array wired as a single-level doubly-linked free list (model, node, sort_z, next 0xc, prev 0x10) and drops the other skip levels. Different data structure, cannot be instruction-compared. Needs a deep audit of the whole render-slot subsystem, this init plus the sort at 0x7F06BB28 plus consumers, with an oracle to confirm the retail extra skip levels are unused.

Rank 209: at 0x7F0ACF4C emits six DL words identical to rank 205's palette: SetRenderMode 0xB900031D and 0x00504240 (G_RM_XLU_SURF, G_RM_XLU_SURF2), SetCombineMode 0xFCFFFFFF and 0xFFFDF6FB (G_CC_PRIMITIVE), SetPrimColor 0xFA000000 and 0, FillRectangle, SetRenderMode again 0xB900031D and 0x00504240, then SetCombineLERP 0xFCFF97FF and 0xFF2DFEFF; returns gdl plus 6. The one structural difference from the live C is that retail DEREFERENCES the four coordinate args as s32 pointers (lw a1, lw a2, lw a3, and lw of the fifth stack arg) to build the FillRectangle: ulx = deref a1, uly = deref a2, lrx = deref a3, lry = deref fifth arg. The port refactored the signature to plain s32 values and updated its NATIVE_PORT call sites to pass values (bondview.c:23134, watch.c:11483, gun.c:31982), while the dead non-NATIVE reference at bondview.c:23136 passes addresses via a (s32) cast of the same locals, i.e. the retail pointer convention. Because the value passed equals what the retail pointer points to, the emitted display list is byte-identical. Verified-equivalent modulo the faithful pointer-to-value signature refactor.

[pull-forward for FID-0080] HUD-bar DL packer, ASM unk_0A1DA0.c:2328-2384 vs live C 2249-2324 (NONMATCHING body, NATIVE_PORT branches), traced fully: half=(arg2/2)-2 signed-div w/ bgez round-toward-zero correction vs C arg2>>1 — differ only for arg2<0 where both yield half<0 and skip the loop (gate bltz/if(half>=0) agrees for all inputs); per-iter G_VTX emit 0x04300040/addr (4 verts, 0x40 bytes, addr stride 0x20) then conditional TRI4 0xB1000032/0x00002110 with cadence i<9: (i&1)==0 (slti/andi/bnez senses verified incl redundant second slti) / i>=9: ((i+3)&3)!=0 (bgez correction dead for i>=9); i s8 wrap via sll/sra 0x18 = C (s8)(i+1); loop bound slt v1,v0 = while(i<=half); addr+=0x20 in loop-back delay slot = per-iteration; tail G_ENDDL 0xB8000000/0 + return gdl+8. NATIVE_PORT words.w0/w1 stores semantically identical to u32 pair stores. CONTAINS NO VERTEX SCALING — never reads/writes vertex data, only emits pointers. FID-0080 implication: in-code rationale 'retail vertex-side scaling in 7F0A3330 unverified' is REFUTED at this fn; any retail compensation for the D_800364CC ortho-extent scale must live in sub_GAME_7F0A2F30's vertex fill (x scale operand = (f32)arg2 with retail callers passing only +/-1 mirror), else FID-0080(a) is a real pixel divergence on 0.2-scale stages.

Rank 211: the LEFTOVERDEBUG variant at 0x7F020794, which IS the port build target (the game defines -DLEFTOVERDEBUG via add_definitions at CMakeLists.txt:1245). Flinch factor from ChrRecord flinchcnt at offset 0x11 (lb, cvt.s.w): if count less than 10.0 returns sinf(count times M_TAU_F times 0.25 over 10.0), else 1.0 minus sinf((count minus 10.0) times M_TAU_F times 0.25 over 20.0). The live NONMATCHING C at chr.c:3591 uses exactly the 10.0 threshold and 20.0 divisor, matching THIS variant. c.lt.s and bc1fl branch sense, the 0.25 constant, and M_TAU_F 0x40c90fdb all match; the header notes only an f0/f2 regalloc swap and a late_rodata offset (9 of 42 instr), not behavior.

Rank 212: the NON-LEFTOVERDEBUG variant of sub_GAME_7F020794 at 0x7F020624, which uses an 8.0 threshold and 16.0 divisor. This is NOT the port build target: the game defines -DLEFTOVERDEBUG (CMakeLists.txt:1237-1248), so the authoritative variant is the LEFTOVERDEBUG one at rank 211 (0x7F020794, 10.0/20.0) which the compiled C matches. This 8.0/16.0 body is the reference for a build the port does not target; verified-equivalent by exclusion (non-target variant), with the faithful match established at rank 211.

Rank 213: at 0x7F0CE7F0 sets rz_inbuf=arg0, rz_outbuf=arg1, rz_hlist=arg2, then a header sniff that reads arg0 byte 0 vs rz_header_bytes index 0 and arg0 byte 1 vs index 5, and only if not both equal reads arg0 byte -1 vs index 8 and byte -2 vs index 24, but stores nothing (the unused local is optimized away; only conditional reloads of rz_inbuf remain), then unconditionally rz_inbuf += 2, rz_wp=0, rz_inptr=0, jal zlib_inflate, returns rz_wp. NATIVE_PORT adds a zlib_inflate return check (sets rz_last_error, returns 0 on failure) that is behavior-identical on success where retail ignores the return.

All colors, offsets, the slt limit guard (dead for valid input), and the sltu loop bound match C. Decomp note match down to regalloc.

Rank 216: off-by-one init bound. Retail obInit at 0x7F0BCC28 fills resource_lookup_data_array with an ascending pointer loop bounded by a1 = base + (file_entry_max-1) times 20 (computed 0x7F0BCC64-0x7F0BCC7C), so it stops after index file_entry_max-2 and never touches index file_entry_max-1; the NATIVE_PORT C at ob.c:168 uses a descending loop from file_entry_max-1 down to 1 and therefore DOES write index file_entry_max-1. Net: the port initializes one extra entry (rom_size plus three zero fields) that retail leaves at its prior value. See FID-0114.

Rank 217: at 0x7F01B134 RLE decoder to RGBA5551. remaining = u16 header word 0 times u16 header word 2; ptr = header+0xa; per entry pixel = ((ptr byte3 shr 3) shl 11) or ((ptr byte2 shr 3) shl 6) or ((ptr byte1 shr 3) shl 1) or 1, remaining -= ptr byte0 (count), ptr += 4; inner do-loop writes the 16-bit pixel count times (sh to s0, s0 += 2) via slt-then-decrement; outer bgtzl while remaining greater than 0. C matches the shifts, packing, offsets, count semantics, and the s0 stack save; reads in order c2,c3,c1,count but all from ptr before it advances.

Rank 218: at 0x7F0A3AB8 setup_watch_rectangles. xval = startx + horizontal_offset (stack arg at sp+0x1c after the -8 prologue); outer i loop 2 iters with xval += width in the delay slot; inner j loop 2 iters with zval = startz + vertical_offset (sp+0x20), writing coord1.z at 0x4 then zval += height (sp+0x18), coord1.x at 0, zeros at 0x2/0x6/0x8/0xa, color bytes 0x20/0x70/0x20/0xF0 at 0xc..0xf, vtx += 0x10 stride. Returns final vtx. All offsets, both loop bounds (bne against s0=2), and constants match C; leaf-function regalloc-only NONMATCHING.

Rank 219: at 0x7F0A3E1C searches dword_CODE_bss_8007A170 (stride 0x2c, 20 entries, end = base+0x370) for the first entry whose unk04 at offset 0x4 (lh) is 0; on hit calls sub_GAME_7F0A3C08(ptr, arg0, arg1, arg2 as float bits via mfc1, arg3 s16 from stack) and returns ptr, else returns NULL. do-while structure, bnezl skip-if-nonzero, sltu ptr less-than-end bound (examines indices 0..19), and the 5-arg marshaling (a2=arg1 preserved, a3=float bits, 5th on stack) all match C. UNK_8007A170_MAX = 20.

Rank 220: at 0x7F0283FC chrlvGetPatrolStepPad. Loads act_patrol.forward (ChrRecord offset 0x34) into a stack local, calls chrlvPatrolCalculateStep(self, and-forward, numsteps) for step, then data = act_patrol.path (offset 0x2c) then deref its data pointer at offset 0 indexed by step; padID = g_CurrentSetup.pathwaypoints (offset 0, stride 0x10) at data, padID field offset 0; returns the address of g_CurrentSetup.pads (offset 0x18) at padID with stride 0x2c synthesized by the shift/sub chain giving padID times 44. All offsets and arg marshaling match C; NONMATCHING is a single-line regalloc swap.

Rank 221: at 0x70009FA8 memaGrow. curr = address of g_MemoryAllocations.spaces index 2 (offset 0x10, stride 8: addr at 0, size at 4). Walks while curr->addr not equal -1; on curr->addr == addr AND curr->size at least amount (sltu size,amount then beql), does curr->addr += amount, curr->size -= amount, and if the new size == 0 sets curr->addr = 0, returns addr; else returns 0. The bnel short-circuit on the addr compare, the beq/bne sentinel checks, and the unsigned size compare all match C. Decomp note regalloc-only.

Rank 222: at 0x7F01B1C8 RLE decoder to RGBA32. remaining = u16 header word 0 times u16 header word 2; ptr = header+0xa; per entry count = ptr byte0, then inner do-loop writes 4 bytes per pixel: out0 = ptr byte3 (c3), out1 = ptr byte2 (c2), out2 = ptr byte1 (c1), out3 = 255, out += 4, repeated count times via slt-then-decrement; remaining -= count, ptr += 4; outer bgtzl while remaining greater than 0. All byte lanes, the 255 constant, offsets, and count semantics match C. Uses v0 for the output pointer directly (no stack frame), matching this variant.

entries.status: waived:asm-only N64 TLB and boot bootstrap at ROM 0x70000450: programs cop0 EntryLo0 EntryHi PageMask Wired via mtc0, runs tlbwi to map the 0x70000000 to 0x70400000 region, then jr to init at 0x70000510. No live C sibling and no port reimplementation; GLOBAL_ASM with no NONMATCHING branch so it is not compiled under NATIVE_PORT. Inherently unportable hardware startup, not sim-reachable (port uses its own host startup). Nothing to instruction-diff. Only revisit if a port TLB emulation layer is ever introduced.

Retail 0x7F027BF4 walks g_CurrentSetup.pathwaypoints (field offset 0, waypoint stride 0x10, padID at waypoint offset 0) until padID goes negative; per entry it indexes pads (g_CurrentSetup field offset 0x18, PadRecord stride 44 bytes) and compares pad stan at PadRecord offset 0x28 against the argument, returning the matching waypoint else NULL, with the NULL-pathwaypoints early-out. Live C chrlv.c matches structure, offsets and both early-outs; the branch-likely next-padID preload equals the for-loop advance-then-test.

Retail 0x7F06B248 prepends arg0 (a node or chain) to the free list D_80036060: null-arg early-out; empty-list stores arg0 and returns; else walks arg0 next-chain (link at offset 0xc) to its tail, sets tail next to the old head, sets old-head prev at offset 0x10 to the tail, then head becomes arg0. This body touches only the 0xc and 0x10 link level, which the NATIVE_PORT single-level ModelRenderSlot free list (next 0xc, prev 0x10) preserves; the dropped higher skip levels flagged at rank 208 are not used here. Live C matches the walk, both early-outs and the old-head prev fixup read before the head store.

Retail 0x7F01B0E0 8-bit indexed RLE decoder: remaining equals u16 width times u16 height (halfword loads then multu), ptr starts at header+0xa, each run reads count and value at ptr and ptr+1 and writes value exactly count times (inner do-while verified for count 1, 2, 3), advancing ptr by 2 and remaining by minus count, looping while remaining stays positive (signed bgtzl). Live C matches. NATIVE_PORT read_be16 reproduces the big-endian halfword loads on little-endian hosts (out of scope). The added null-header-or-output early return has no retail counterpart but only turns a retail null-deref crash into a clean return; identical for every input retail survives.

Retail 0x7F06C010 walks the next-chain (link at offset 0xc) from the head node to the last node, stores it back to the head slot, zeroes the two output pointers, and tail-calls probably_damage_detail_blood_effect_related passing head, the two unused params, and the fourth output as the fifth stack argument. Live NATIVE_PORT C matches the traversal (ModelRenderSlot next at 0xc), the two zeroing stores and the forwarded call. The added head-null early return (return 0, zero the outputs, no call) has no retail counterpart, but retail would null-deref reading offset 0xc of a null head; identical for every non-null head. Same single-level free list as rank 225.

Retail 0x7F09B740 is instruction-for-instruction identical to randomGetNext but on g_chrObjRandomSeed: t equals seed left-63 right-31 or seed left-31 right-32, xor seed left-44 right-32, new seed equals t xor ((t right-20) and 0xfff), returning the low 32 bits sign-extended via dsll32 then dsra32. Live C matches exactly.

Retail 0x7F0004D0 relocates a struct animation table array (stride 0x1c, anonymous_0 at offset 0): while anonymous_0 is nonzero it adds ptr_animation_table to it in place, increments count and advances; returns count, guarding on the first element before the loop. Live C matches. NATIVE_PORT uses uintptr_t 64-bit for the relocation add versus the retail 32-bit addu, the correct host pointer-width accommodation, byte-identical for 32-bit offsets.

Retail 0x7000A450 iterates g_randomSeed: t equals seed left-63 right-31 or seed left-31 right-32, xor seed left-44 right-32, new seed equals t xor ((t right-20) and 0xfff), stored and returned as the low 32 bits sign-extended. Live C core math matches exactly. The surrounding NATIVE_PORT instrumentation (call counting, RNG trace, scripted call-seed injection) is env-gated and a default no-op, leaving the seed math byte-identical.
Retail 0x70001B10 is identical to randomGetNext but on g_tlbRandomSeed and returns s32 via dsll32 then dsra32 sign-extension of the low 32 bits. Live C matches exactly.

Retail 0x7000A4AC applies the randomGetNext iteration to a caller-supplied u64 pointer instead of the global: loads the value, computes t equals v left-63 right-31 or v left-31 right-32 xor v left-44 right-32, new equals t xor ((t right-20) and 0xfff), stores it back and returns the low 32 bits sign-extended. Live C matches exactly.

Portal-chain room resolver. jpt_80058C58 camera-mode switch sets D_800442F8 to 200 for modes 1,2,3,7,9 else 3 (index cam-1 bound 9). Inner portal loop: bg_portal_data_entry stride 8, offset_portal at struct offset 0, connectedRoom1 at 4, connectedRoom2 at 5. Guards flags at D_800443C4 equal 0 and portal_idx not equal last_portal; side test current_room equals connectedRoom1 or connectedRoom2; on sub_GAME_7F0B9F14 hit sets last_portal and current_room = connectedRoom2 xor (current_room xor connectedRoom1). Both break conditions and the 11-iteration outer bound match. NATIVE_PORT block is a comment plus a default-off GE007_FORCE_NEARBY_ROOMS debug override.

Model-node tree walker. Init picks node from *arg3 (then clears it) else model->obj->RootNode; ModelNode fields Opcode halfword at 0, Data at 4, Parent at 8, Next at 0xc, Child at 0x14. Child-descend then Next/Parent ascent match. type = Opcode and 0xFF; jpt_80054DB4 index type-1 bound 0x18 dispatches cases 8 (modelApplyDistanceRelations), 10 (sub_GAME_7F074C68), 17 (sub_GAME_7F074CAC), 18 (modelApplyToggleRelations), 23 (modelApplyHeadRelations), all else default. Cases 10 and 17 on hit set *arg3=node and return *(s32*)node->Data (offset 4). bboxTrace diagnostics and uintptr_t arg casts are NATIVE_PORT-only, sim-neutral.

Sphere/capsule-vs-ray hit test, faithful transcription verified against ASM 7F074CAC. node->Data at 4, BoundingVolumeRadius at Data+4, Op17 pos at Data+8, RelatedNode at Data+0x14, Scale1 at 0x18, Scale2 at 0x1c. opcode = node->Opcode at 0; flag dispatch 0x100 rotated pos, 0x200 child-node midpoint (0.5 average) else plain diff = mtx row3 minus arg2; then 0x400 double scale1/scale2 projections onto mtx rows 0 and 1, else single-axis 0x800/0x1000/0x2000 or skip to final. Final sphere-ray test is division-free cross-multiplication: dot = dir3 dot diff, gated on 0 < dot, then (lendiff minus radius) times lendir less-equal dotsq returns 1. NATIVE_PORT mtx-null early-outs and bboxTrace are sim-neutral. Contrasts sibling rank 245 which was rewritten with division.

Ray-vs-OBB slab test. Retail ASM 7F0747D0 is division-free (only mul.s/add.s/sub.s/neg.s/c.lt.s with near/far swaps, zero div.s instructions), carrying axis_len_sq and local_dir as denominators into cross-multiplied comparisons. The port reimplemented it with explicit floating-point division plus a 1e-6f parallel-ray epsilon absent in retail. Filed as FID-0116: bit-exactness at grazing angles and degenerate axes is unproven, and the routine is on the combat path (chrobjhandler.c bullet-vs-object and bullet-vs-door hit tests with shot_pos and shot_dir).

Animation ticker, core verified against ASM 7F070AEC. frame from model unk28, frame2acc from unk58, ticks-less-equal-0 early exit. Per-tick: unkb0 playspeed ramp (playspeed at 0xa4, animrate at 0xa8, unkac at 0xac, unkb0/unkb4 at 0xb0/0xb4) with div.s matching retail; unk88 merge sets anim2 null at 0x54; timespeed interp (speed 0x40, oldspeed 0x44, newspeed 0x48, elapsespeed 0x50); frame += playspeed times speed; anim2 speed2 accumulation; animlooping at 0x26 equal 0 continues; loop-flip calls modelSetAnimation then final modelSetAnimFrame2 or WithChrStuff. Division in the ramps is faithful (retail uses div.s). NATIVE_PORT portChrActionNeedsLooping loop-restore and frame-value guards are documented compat fixes.

Rank 253 per-room collision bbox builder traced vs ASM 7F0B6994. Pass 1 counts G_VTX (type 4, end 0xB8); memaAlloc quadCount times 0x1C aligned 16; writes ptr_unique_collision_points at 0x2c and bitflags3 count at 0x30. Pass 2 per quad: cmdIndex s16 at rec 0, numVerts from cmd byte1 upper nibble plus 1, vtxOffset from big-endian cmd bytes 4-7 masked 0xFFFFFF, per-vertex s16 x/y/z bbox stride 0x10 init 0x7FFF/0xFFFF8000, min==max bump, room-position add from ptr_bgdata_room_fileposition_list pos truncated to s32, 6 bbox s32 at rec 0x04-0x18. All match. Port malloc+memset (zeroes unused pad 0x02), big-endian reassembly and null-checks are sim-neutral.

Rank 254 portal-crossing test traced vs ASM 7F0B9F14. dir=arg2-arg1; dot1/dot2 = arg dot normal times room_data_float1; same-side reject; D_80044900 = midpoint minus metric.min; per-edge cross product of edge and dir with mag_sq==0 reject; vert_dot; arg1_dot with the arg1.z times cross.z operand-order quirk; flag_pos/flag_neg consistency reject; return (dot1<min)?1:2 all match. Sole divergence is latent: at numPoints==0 retail returns (dot1<min)?1:2 (blezl skips loop to 7F0BA238) while NATIVE_PORT returns 0; dead because BG portals always have at least 3 vertices. NATIVE_PORT bgGetPortalEntryPtr null-checks are added hardening.

Rank 255 portal-vertex projection and near-plane clip traced vs ASM 7F0B5528. Per vertex: read portal vert offsets 4/8/0xc, optional metric.normal times offset add when offset nonzero (sub_GAME_7F0B96CC), times room_data_float2, mtx4TransformVecInPlace; all-behind test -maxZ times 0.9 (rodata D_80058C7C) less than cur z clears allBehind, maxZ = zrange 1 over mCurrentLevelVisibilityScale. Near-plane clip t = -curZ over (nextZ minus curZ) interpolates x and y with z=0. numPoints<=0 returns 0 via allBehind on both. Match. NATIVE_PORT null-checks added.

Rank 256 traced vs ASM 7F0B7A78. Core nearest-hit matches: scale by room_data_float1, per-quad bgTestLineIntersectsBbox cull then bgTestLineIntersectionInRoom, dist_sq of integer-truncated hit minus start, 4-unit dist bias when check_if_imageID_is_light, best-distance field copy to output (0-0x14 f32, 0x18/0x1c/0x20 s32, 0x24, 0x28 s16, 0x2a texturenum). Divergence filed FID-0119: retail tail 7F0B7D2C-7F0B7D5C derives texture_s at output 0x2c and texture_t at 0x2e from a backward DL scan to the 0xF5 command; NATIVE_PORT hardcodes both to -1 because native bgTestLineIntersectionInRoom (rank 240) has no DL pointer at 0x24.

Rank 259 portal-bbox room flood-fill traced vs ASM 7F0BA2D4. propbbmin/max = arg bbox times room_data_float1. Per source room, per portal: skip if controlbytes1 bit 0, dest_room = source==room1?room2 : source==room2?room1 : skip, portal bbox init from FLT_MAX globals D_80044904 and -FLT_MAX D_80044910 over all points equals the C point-0 init over the rest, bgIsBboxOverlapping, dedup then arg2 count++ guarded by count<arg4. Two non-obvious equivalences hold: sentinel-init equals point-0 init for real coords; the C do-while reset-to-0 pass equals the ASM monotonic queue walk because the dedup makes reprocessing idempotent and preserves append order. NATIVE_PORT null/bounds/count-clamp are hardening.

ModelNode matrix processor (euler-angle variant). parent_mtx via modelFindNodeMtx or arg0 unk_matrix; main set_position_and_rotation_around_xyz then multiply_homogeneous into base index_e pos matches 7F06D8B0-D958. The 0x100 half-quat block (set_rotation_around_xyzf, 7F05BC68 at 0.5, to_transform_matrix) and 0x200 scale block (angle halved about PI, set_rotation_around_y, 7F05A9E0 else 1.5, column-3 mult, set_position, conditional multiply) match 7F06D99C-DB58 branch-for-branch incl delay-slot reuse. g_aimBoneCallback replaces raw D_80036090 hook; portTraceGuardMatrixWrite inert; both NATIVE_PORT only.

ModelNode matrix processor (quaternion variant). Main uses quaternion_to_transform_matrix(data, quat, ...) matching 7F06DBDC-DC44; 0x200 block computes angle = acosf(quat0)*2 via jal acosf then add.s f0,f0 (x+x equals x*2.0 exactly in IEEE) matching 7F06DCF0-DCFC. Half-quat 0x100 block and scale block identical to the 7F06D8B0 sibling. g_aimBoneCallback plus portTraceGuard NATIVE_PORT only.

S5 batch 22 rank 264: stan-branch omits retail firststaninroom NULL guard (retail returns early at 7F0B939C when firststaninroom[room] is 0; port bg.c:18130-18144 only gates on room >= dword_CODE_bss_8007B9DC). Stan arithmetic and the geometry BE vertex min/max scan otherwise match instruction-for-instruction. See FID-0120.

ModelNode matrix processor (scalar rotation-around-y variant, 4th arg f32 value in f20). parent_mtx select then set_position_and_rotation_around_y into base index_e pos matches 7F06E2B8-E384. The combined has_bit8-or-has_bit9 value-halving block re-reads has_bit9 = opcode and 0x200 inside the if (andi at 7F06E3B4), and set_rotation_around_y receives value through the beqz delay-slot mov.s f12,f20 in both parent branches; both subtleties reproduced. Scale block (>= PI fold, 7F05A9E0 else 1.5, column-3 mult, set_position, conditional multiply) matches 7F06E464-E528. portTraceGuard NATIVE_PORT only.

8-corner 6-plane room AABB frustum cull. NATIVE_PORT body re-derives 7F0B5208 register-for-register: maxZ = zrange1 / mCurrentLevelVisibilityScale, 8 corners via corner_idx bit pattern selecting minbounds when bit set else maxbounds, 7F0B5488 projects (returns 1 if behind). Counters far=s1 left=s2 right=s3 top=s4 bottom=s5 behind=s6. Front branch (5300-538C) bumps far/left/right/top/bottom conditionally plus behind unconditionally; behind branch (5390-5418) uses inverted else-if comparisons and omits behind. Return 0 if any count equals 8 else 1 matches 542C-5460 same short-circuit order. The return-1 stub is dead (FID-0010 refuted); this NONMATCHING plus NATIVE_PORT body is live.

portal plane-normal plus min/max dot. NATIVE_PORT re-derives 7F0B96CC: edge1 = pts0..2 minus pts3..5 and edge2 = pts6..8 minus pts3..5 via two unrolled subtract loops (970C-974C, 9768-97A4), cross-product nx/ny/nz stored to arg1 normal as scratch (97A8-9808), mag = sqrtf(nx2+ny2+nz2) with invmag = 1/mag or mag when zero (983C-985C), normal scaled in place. Dot loop over numPoints (byte at portal 0, pts at portal+4, 12-byte stride) seeds FLT_MAX and -FLT_MAX (D_80058D54/58), writes arg1 min at 0xc and max at 0x10 (98C8-9924). Dot terms differ by IEEE-commutative reassociation only.

entries.status: waived:PC room model loader -- full NATIVE_PORT architectural reimplementation. Retail 7F0B6368 loads compressed room vertex/DL binaries from ROM into fixed N64 memory banks; the port at bg.c:10020-10208 malloc's PC_ROOM_LOAD_BUF_SIZE buffers, decompresses via 7F0B5FAC/609C/61DC, registers big-endian N64 DL regions for the GBI translator, then applies CCRM LUT fog rewrites and light-fixture repopulation. Memory-model divergence is intentional and not instruction-diffable. Retest if a room-load-side-effect oracle is authored.

ModelRwData header-record position update. modelGetNodeRwData then early-out if unk00 != 0; target = unk34, unk14 = unk30; when model unk2c != 0 and rwdata unk01 != 0 does interpolate3dVectors(&target, unk24, unk2c) and unk14 = 7F06D0CC(unk30, unk20, unk2c); when model anim2 or unk84 nonzero and rwdata unk02 != 0 blends target.y toward unk4c.y (optionally toward unk40.y by unk5c) by unk84. local_pos = target; optional model unka0 callback(model, rwdata pos, local_pos, rwdata ground) early-outs on 0; then delta = local_pos minus target, writes pos (y plus ground), unk34 plus delta, and unk01/unk02-gated unk24/unk4c/unk40 plus delta. entries.status: waived:full NATIVE_PORT architectural reimplementation. Retail 7F06C094 manages a fixed 32-byte-per-slot Model pool (ptr_allocation_1): scans for a free slot (slot 8 field is 0) or allocates 32 bytes via mempAllocBytesInBank, allocates rwdata sized numRecords times 4 aligned to 16 from a bank, then modelInit and writes the saved OISAVESIZE index to slot 2. The port at model.c:766-794 instead calloc's an individual 248-byte pointer-expanded Model and a 2x-sized rwdata array with no pool reuse, because the expanded struct cannot fit the N64 32-byte slot. Documented memory/pointer-size divergence, not instruction-diffable. Retest if a model-pool-reuse oracle is authored.
